task_completion.description: The Shader Editor is open and visible, showing the material node graph for CarBody.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender Shader Editor workspace is visible in the main window area, displaying shader nodes for the CarBody material.

task_completion.description: A Noise Texture node exists in the shader graph with Scale approximately 5.0 and Detail approximately 8.0.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Noise Texture' node is visible in the Shader Editor. Its Scale field shows a value near 5.0 and Detail field shows a value near 8.0.

task_completion.description: A Color Ramp node exists and is connected to receive output from the Noise Texture.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Color Ramp' node is visible in the Shader Editor with a black-to-white gradient. A noodle (wire) connects from the Noise Texture's 'Fac' output to the Color Ramp's 'Fac' input.

task_completion.description: Two separate Principled BSDF nodes exist: one configured as dark metal (Metallic 1.0, low Roughness) and one as rust (brown color, Metallic 0.0, high Roughness).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Two distinct Principled BSDF nodes are visible. One shows a dark grey Base Color with Metallic at or near 1.0 and Roughness near 0.2. The other shows a brown/orange Base Color with Metallic at 0.0 and Roughness near 0.8.

task_completion.description: A Mix Shader node blends the two BSDFs, with the Color Ramp output driving the Factor input.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Mix Shader' node is visible with noodles connecting: Color Ramp output to the Mix Shader's 'Fac' input, one Principled BSDF to the first Shader input, and the other Principled BSDF to the second Shader input. The Mix Shader output connects to the Material Output's Surface input.

task_completion.description: A Voronoi Texture is connected through a Bump node to provide surface normal detail for corrosion pitting.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Voronoi Texture' node connects to a 'Bump' node's Height input. The Bump node's Normal output connects to the Normal input of at least one Principled BSDF node.

task_completion.description: The procedural rust material is applied to the car body and visible in the 3D viewport.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewport (in Material Preview or Rendered mode) shows the BMW car body with a patchy appearance of dark metallic areas interspersed with brown/orange rust patches. The pattern is clearly procedural and noise-driven.

task_completion.description: A render of the scene has been completed showing the rust material on the car.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender Render window is visible showing a completed render of the BMW car with the procedural rust material clearly visible -- dark metallic and brown rust areas with surface bump detail.

task_completion.description: The rendered image is saved to /home/ga/BlenderProjects/rust_render.png.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file save dialog shows 'rust_render.png' being saved to '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/', or a file manager confirms the file exists at that path.
The Blender title bar shows 'rust_bmw.blend', or a 'Save As' dialog confirms saving to '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/rust_bmw.blend'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Blender Shader Editor GUI to build the node graph.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active use of the Shader Editor with nodes being added via the Add menu (Shift+A), positioned, and connected by dragging noodles between sockets, rather than executing a Python script in a text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: Nodes were created individually through the GUI, not via a bulk scripting approach.
integrity.visual_evidence: Sequential screenshots show the node graph being built incrementally -- nodes appear one at a time across multiple steps, with the agent searching for and selecting each node type from the Add menu.

integrity.description: The render was performed through Blender's Render menu or F12 shortcut.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Blender Render window shows rendering statistics (Time, Mem, Peak, or visible rendering tiles) confirming the image was actually computed by the Cycles engine.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target object: 'CarBody' (the car body shell mesh, originally named 'carShell' in BMW27.blend, renamed by setup). The material should show approximately 60% clean dark metallic surface (#2A2A2A, Metallic 1.0, Roughness 0.2) and 40% brown/orange rust (#8B4513, Metallic 0.0, Roughness 0.8). The rust pattern is generated by a Noise Texture (Scale 5.0, Detail 8.0) fed through a Color Ramp with threshold at position 0.4. Corrosion pitting detail comes from a Voronoi Texture (Scale 15.0) through a Bump node (Strength 0.3) connected to both BSDFs' Normal inputs. Required node types in the shader graph: Noise Texture, Color Ramp, Principled BSDF (x2), Mix Shader, Voronoi Texture, Bump. Expected outputs: /home/ga/BlenderProjects/rust_render.png (rendered image, min 50KB) and /home/ga/BlenderProjects/rust_bmw.blend (project file).
